_note: Rules for classifying activation strength. A phoneme can activate a macro-concept through three tiers. This prevents the 'everything activates everything' problem caused by broad sound groups.

strong.rule: The phoneme is one of the macro-concept's primary_cardinal_sounds
strong.description: This phoneme statistically drove the association in the original cross-linguistic data

medium.rule: The phoneme matches a SPECIFIC sound group (one with a place+voicing qualifier, e.g., alveolar_voiced, nasal_voiced, labial_voiceless) but is NOT a primary cardinal sound
medium.description: The phoneme shares specific articulatory features with the primary drivers

weak.rule: The phoneme matches only a BROAD sound group (voiced, voiceless, rounded_vowel, unrounded_vowel, front_vowel, back_vowel, low_vowel, stop, nasal, labial)
weak.description: The phoneme shares only general features — this match is likely noise

_classification_algorithm: For each (phoneme, macro-concept) pair where there IS an overlap between sound_groups: 1) Check if phoneme is in macro-concept's primary_cardinal_sounds → STRONG. 2) Else check if the overlap includes any specific_sound_group → MEDIUM. 3) Else (overlap is only broad groups) → WEAK.
_primary_cardinal_mapping_note: The primary_cardinal_sounds in erben_table.json use simplified phoneme labels (e.g., 'k', 'o'). These need to be matched against IPA phoneme IDs. For Hungarian: 'a' in primary_cardinal_sounds means /a/ (the open vowel, corresponding to Hungarian á /aː/), NOT Hungarian a /ɒ/. 'o' means /o/ (matches Hungarian o and ó). 'u' means /u/ (matches Hungarian u and ú). 'i' means /i/ (matches Hungarian i and í). 'r' means /r/, 'l' means /l/, etc.

_cardinal_to_ipa_map._note: Cardinal 'a' maps to Hungarian á /aː/ and e /ɛ/ (both low-front unrounded). Cardinal 'n' includes ɲ (palatal nasal, still nasal_voiced). Lengths don't affect classification.
